protocol.h(3) Library Functions Manual protocol.h(3)

NAME

protocol.h - ascii-chat Client Protocol Handler Interface

SYNOPSIS

Functions


int protocol_start_connection ()
Start protocol connection handling. void protocol_stop_connection ()
Stop protocol connection handling. bool protocol_connection_lost ()
Check if connection has been lost. const acip_client_callbacks_t * protocol_get_acip_callbacks ()
Get ACIP client callbacks for packet dispatch.

Detailed Description

ascii-chat Client Protocol Handler Interface

Defines the protocol handling interface for client-side packet processing and data reception thread management.

The protocol handler manages the data reception thread, which continuously receives packets from the server, decrypts them, and dispatches them to appropriate handlers based on packet type. It also detects connection loss and signals the main thread to initiate reconnection.

Command Types Handled

The client processes these command/packet types from the server:

  • PACKET_TYPE_ASCII_FRAME: Terminal display frame (rendered to stdout)
  • PACKET_TYPE_AUDIO_BATCH: Opus-encoded audio samples (sent to audio playback)
  • PACKET_TYPE_PONG: Keepalive response (recorded for timeout detection)
  • PACKET_TYPE_CLEAR_CONSOLE: Terminal clear command (resets display)
  • PACKET_TYPE_SERVER_STATE: Server state updates (logged for debugging)

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

2025

See also

topic_client_protocol "Protocol Architecture"

Definition in file src/client/protocol.h.

Function Documentation

bool protocol_connection_lost ()

Check if connection has been lost. Atomically checks whether the data reception thread has detected connection loss. Connection loss is detected by:

  • Socket read error (recv() returns error)
  • Socket closed by server (recv() returns 0)
  • Decryption failure (corrupted stream)
  • Invalid packet magic number (out of sync)

Once loss is detected, this flag remains true until the next successful connection.

Returns

true if connection lost (detected by protocol thread), false otherwise

Note

This is checked by the main thread to trigger reconnection logic. The flag is set atomically by the protocol thread and checked atomically by main.

See also

server_connection_lost "Signal connection loss (called by protocol thread)"

Check if connection has been lost

Returns

true if protocol detected connection loss, false otherwise

Definition at line 1435 of file client/protocol.c.

1435                                 {
1436   return atomic_load_bool(&g_data_thread_exited) || server_connection_is_lost();
1437 }




References atomic_load_bool(), and
    server_connection_is_lost().






const
  acip_client_callbacks_t * protocol_get_acip_callbacks ()


Get ACIP client callbacks for packet dispatch. Returns the packet
    dispatch callbacks structure used to route received packets to appropriate
    handlers. This is primarily used for ACDS signaling packet dispatch and
    WebRTC peer negotiation.




The callbacks structure includes handlers for:


    
  
  • ASCII frame rendering
    • 
  
  • Audio batch processing
    • 
  
  • Server state updates
    • 
  
  • Connection control commands (clear, reset, etc.)
    • 



Returns


Pointer to client callbacks structure (never NULL)


Used by WebRTC sessions to receive and dispatch ACDS signaling
    packets.




Returns pointer to the global callback structure for use by WebRTC
    sessions that need to receive ACDS signaling packets.




Returns


Pointer to client callbacks (never NULL)


Definition at line 982 of file
  client/protocol.c.


982                                                              {
983   return &g_acip_client_callbacks;
984 }










int
  protocol_start_connection ()


Start protocol connection handling. Spawns the data reception
    thread in the global client worker thread pool. The thread begins receiving
    and processing packets from the server. Must be called after the server
    connection is established and the cryptographic handshake is complete.




The thread will:



  
1.

  
Continuously receive packets from the server socket

  
2.

  
Decrypt packets using the negotiated session key

  
3.

  
Dispatch packets to appropriate handlers based on type

  
4.

  
Detect connection loss and signal the main thread




Returns


0 on success, negative on error (thread pool full or
  other resources exhausted)


Note


Must be paired with protocol_stop_connection() to
  properly shut down the thread before reconnection or exit.


See also


protocol_stop_connection "Stop data reception
  thread"



protocol_connection_lost "Check if connection was
    lost"




Start protocol connection handling




Initializes protocol state and starts the data reception thread.
    Must be called after successful server connection establishment.




Returns


0 on success, negative on error


Definition at line 1180 of file
  client/protocol.c.


1180                                 {
1181   log_warn("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] 🟢 PROTOCOL_START: Starting client protocol initialization");
1182 
1183   // Register protocol atomics with named debug registry
1184   static bool protocol_atomics_registered = false;
1185   if (!protocol_atomics_registered) {
1186     NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&g_data_thread_exited, "protocol_data_reception_thread_exit_confirmation", NULL);
1187     NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&g_frames_rendered, "protocol_frames_successfully_rendered", NULL);
1188     protocol_atomics_registered = true;
1189   }
1190 
1191   // Reset protocol state for new connection
1192   g_server_state_initialized = false;
1193   g_last_active_count = 0;
1194   g_should_clear_before_next_frame = false;
1195 
1196   log_info("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] ✅ STATE_RESET: server_initialized=false, active_count=0, clear_flag=false");
1197 
1198   // Reset display state for new connection
1199   display_reset_for_new_connection();
1200 
1201   // Check if we're on a WebSocket transport (crypto handshake is async via ACIP callbacks)
1202   // For WS/WSS, we must start data_reception FIRST so it can handle the crypto handshake,
1203   // then wait for crypto to complete before sending CAPABILITIES/STREAM_START.
1204   // For TCP, crypto is already done synchronously before this function is called.
1205   acip_transport_t *init_transport = server_connection_get_transport();
1206   bool is_websocket = init_transport &&
1207                       acip_transport_get_type(init_transport) == ACIP_TRANSPORT_WEBSOCKET;
1208 
1209   if (is_websocket) {
1210     // WebSocket: start data reception thread FIRST to handle async crypto handshake.
1211     // TCP does crypto synchronously in connection_attempt.c, but WS crypto flows
1212     // asynchronously via ACIP callbacks in the data_reception thread.
1213     log_debug("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] WS: Starting data_reception for async crypto handshake");
1214     atomic_store_bool(&g_data_thread_exited, false);
1215     if (thread_pool_spawn(g_client_worker_pool, data_reception_thread_func, NULL, 1, "data_reception") != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1216       log_error("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] Failed to start data reception thread");
1217       LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Data reception thread creation failed");
1218       return -1;
1219     }
1220     log_debug("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] WS: Data thread spawned, waiting for crypto handshake");
1221 
1222     // Wait for crypto handshake to complete (CRYPTO_PARAMETERS → KEY_EXCHANGE → HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE)
1223     // Skip waiting if encryption is disabled (no_encrypt mode)
1224     if (!GET_OPTION(no_encrypt)) {
1225       const int MAX_CRYPTO_WAIT_MS = 10000; // 10 seconds
1226       const int POLL_INTERVAL_MS = 10;
1227       int waited_ms = 0;
1228       while (!crypto_client_is_ready() && !should_exit() && server_connection_is_active() &&
1229              waited_ms < MAX_CRYPTO_WAIT_MS) {
1230         platform_sleep_us(POLL_INTERVAL_MS * 1000);
1231         waited_ms += POLL_INTERVAL_MS;
1232         if (waited_ms % 1000 == 0) {
1233           log_info("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] WS_CRYPTO: Waiting for handshake... (%d ms elapsed)", waited_ms);
1234         }
1235       }
1236 
1237       if (!crypto_client_is_ready()) {
1238         if (should_exit() || !server_connection_is_active()) {
1239           log_error("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] WS: Connection lost during crypto handshake");
1240         } else {
1241           log_error("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] WS: Crypto handshake timed out after %d ms", MAX_CRYPTO_WAIT_MS);
1242         }
1243         return -1;
1244       }
1245       log_info("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] WS: Crypto handshake completed after %d ms", waited_ms);
1246     } else {
1247       log_info("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] WS: Encryption disabled, skipping handshake wait");
1248     }
1249   }
1250 
1251   // Send CLIENT_CAPABILITIES packet (after crypto is ready for both TCP and WS)
1252   // Server expects this as the first packet after crypto handshake
1253   log_debug("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] Sending CLIENT_CAPABILITIES (terminal size)");
1254   asciichat_error_t cap_result = threaded_send_terminal_size_with_auto_detect((int)terminal_get_effective_width(),
1255                                                                               (int)terminal_get_effective_height());
1256   if (cap_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1257     log_error("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] Failed to send terminal size capabilities");
1258     return -1;
1259   }
1260 
1261   // Send STREAM_START packet with combined stream types
1262   uint32_t stream_types = STREAM_TYPE_VIDEO; // Always have video
1263   if (GET_OPTION(audio_enabled)) {
1264     stream_types |= STREAM_TYPE_AUDIO; // Add audio if enabled
1265   }
1266   log_debug("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] Sending STREAM_START: types=0x%x (video%s)", stream_types,
1267             (stream_types & STREAM_TYPE_AUDIO) ? "+audio" : "");
1268   asciichat_error_t stream_result = threaded_send_stream_start_packet(stream_types);
1269   if (stream_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1270     log_error("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] Failed to send stream start");
1271     return -1;
1272   }
1273 
1274   // Start data reception thread (TCP only - WS already started it above for crypto)
1275   if (!is_websocket) {
1276     log_debug("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] TCP: Starting data reception thread");
1277     atomic_store_bool(&g_data_thread_exited, false);
1278     if (thread_pool_spawn(g_client_worker_pool, data_reception_thread_func, NULL, 1, "data_reception") != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1279       log_error("[FRAME_RECV_INIT] Failed to start data reception thread");
1280       LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Data reception thread creation failed");
1281       return -1;
1282     }
1283   }
1284 
1285   // Start webcam capture thread
1286   log_debug("Starting webcam capture thread...");
1287   if (capture_start_thread() != 0) {
1288     log_error("Failed to start webcam capture thread");
1289     return -1;
1290   }
1291   log_debug("Webcam capture thread started successfully");
1292 
1293   // Initialize audio sender thread BEFORE starting audio capture
1294   // This ensures sender is ready when capture thread starts queueing packets
1295   // Must happen after connection succeeds to prevent deadlock if connection fails
1296   audio_sender_init();
1297 
1298   // Start audio capture thread if audio is enabled
1299   log_debug("Starting audio capture thread...");
1300   if (audio_start_thread() != 0) {
1301     log_error("Failed to start audio capture thread");
1302     return -1;
1303   }
1304   log_debug("Audio capture thread started successfully (or skipped if audio disabled)");
1305 
1306   // Start keepalive/ping thread to prevent server timeout
1307   log_debug("Starting keepalive/ping thread...");
1308   if (keepalive_start_thread() != 0) {
1309     log_error("Failed to start keepalive/ping thread");
1310     return -1;
1311   }
1312   log_debug("Keepalive/ping thread started successfully");
1313 
1314   g_data_thread_created = true;
1315   return 0;
1316 }






References ACIP_TRANSPORT_WEBSOCKET, ASCIICHAT_OK,
    atomic_store_bool(), audio_sender_init(),
    audio_start_thread(), capture_start_thread(),
    crypto_client_is_ready(), display_reset_for_new_connection(),
    g_client_worker_pool, GET_OPTION,
    keepalive_start_thread(), log_debug, LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET,
    log_error, log_info, log_warn,
    NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC, platform_sleep_us(),
    server_connection_get_transport(),
    server_connection_is_active(), should_exit(),
    STREAM_TYPE_AUDIO, STREAM_TYPE_VIDEO,
    terminal_get_effective_height(),
    terminal_get_effective_width(), thread_pool_spawn(),
    threaded_send_stream_start_packet(), and
    threaded_send_terminal_size_with_auto_detect().






void
  protocol_stop_connection ()


Stop protocol connection handling. Signals the data reception
    thread to exit and waits for it to join. Performs proper cleanup to prevent
    resource leaks and race conditions during reconnection or shutdown.




The function:



  
1.

  
Closes the server socket (interrupts blocking recv() in data thread)

  
2.

  
Sets the global shutdown flag (signals thread to exit)

  
3.

  
Waits for the data reception thread to join (polls for thread exit)




This must be called before reconnecting to prevent socket
    descriptor leaks and to ensure the thread has fully cleaned up before
  reuse.




Note


This function blocks until the data reception thread
  exits. Typical wait time: <100ms (socket closure immediately interrupts
  recv()).


See also


protocol_start_connection "Start data
  reception thread"


Stop protocol connection handling




Gracefully shuts down the data reception thread and cleans up
    protocol state. Safe to call multiple times.


Definition at line 1326 of file
  client/protocol.c.


1326                                 {
1327   // Log final frame count before shutting down (always log for diagnostic purposes)
1328   int final_frame_count = atomic_load_u64(&g_frames_rendered);
1329   log_info("📊 CLIENT SESSION STATS: %d unique frames rendered during connection", final_frame_count);
1330 
1331   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 1. Starting protocol_stop_connection");
1332 
1333   // In snapshot mode, data reception thread was never started, but capture thread may still be running
1334   // Always stop the capture thread to prevent use-after-free when transport is destroyed
1335   if (GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode)) {
1336     log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] Snapshot mode: flushing H.265 encoder before stopping capture thread");
1337     // Flush the encoder to output any buffered frames before exiting
1338     if (g_h265_encoder) {
1339       // Allocate buffer large enough for worst-case H.265 encoded frame
1340       // Using 320x240 (typical test resolution) * 2x multiplier + header
1341       size_t flush_buf_size = 5 + (320 * 240 * 2);
1342       uint8_t *flush_buf = SAFE_MALLOC(flush_buf_size, uint8_t *);
1343       size_t flush_size = flush_buf_size;
1344       asciichat_error_t flush_result = h265_encoder_flush(g_h265_encoder, flush_buf, &flush_size);
1345       if (flush_result == ASCIICHAT_OK && flush_size > 0) {
1346         log_info("[PROTOCOL_STOP] Flushed H.265 encoder: got %zu bytes of buffered frames", flush_size);
1347         // Send the flushed frame if we got one
1348         acip_transport_t *transport = server_connection_get_transport();
1349         if (transport && server_connection_is_active()) {
1350           uint32_t width = 320, height = 240; // TODO: track actual dimensions
1351           log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] Sending flushed frame: %ux%u, %zu bytes", width, height, flush_size);
1352         }
1353       }
1354       SAFE_FREE(flush_buf);
1355     }
1356     log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] Snapshot mode: stopping capture thread before returning");
1357     capture_stop_thread();
1358     return;
1359   }
1360 
1361   // Don't call signal_exit() here - that's for global shutdown only!
1362   // We just want to stop threads for this connection, not exit the entire client
1363 
1364   // Shutdown the socket FIRST to interrupt any blocking network operations in worker threads
1365   // This must happen BEFORE audio_stop_thread() so audio sender thread unblocks from network send
1366   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 2. About to call server_connection_shutdown");
1367   server_connection_shutdown();
1368   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 3. server_connection_shutdown() returned");
1369 
1370   // Signal audio sender thread to exit
1371   // Must happen after socket shutdown so any blocked network calls fail
1372   // Audio sender is created in ALL modes except snapshot mode
1373   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 4. About to call audio_stop_thread");
1374   audio_stop_thread();
1375   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 5. audio_stop_thread() returned");
1376 
1377   // Early return if data thread was never created (e.g., mirror mode)
1378   // In mirror mode, we only need to stop the audio sender (done above)
1379   if (!g_data_thread_created) {
1380     log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 6. Data thread not created, returning");
1381     return;
1382   }
1383 
1384   // Stop keepalive/ping thread - it checks connection status and will exit
1385   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 7. About to call keepalive_stop_thread");
1386   keepalive_stop_thread();
1387   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 8. keepalive_stop_thread() returned");
1388 
1389   // Stop webcam capture thread
1390   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 9. About to call capture_stop_thread");
1391   capture_stop_thread();
1392   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 10. capture_stop_thread() returned");
1393 
1394   // Wait for data reception thread to exit gracefully
1395   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 11. Waiting for data thread to exit");
1396   // Thread checks should_exit() every read cycle (typically <1-5ms), so timeout can be much shorter
1397   int wait_count = 0;
1398   while (wait_count < 5 && !atomic_load_bool(&g_data_thread_exited)) {
1399     platform_sleep_us(10 * US_PER_MS_INT); // 10ms * 5 = 50ms max wait
1400     wait_count++;
1401   }
1402 
1403   if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_data_thread_exited)) {
1404     log_warn("Data thread not responding after 50ms - will be joined by thread pool");
1405   }
1406   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 12. Data thread wait complete");
1407 
1408   // Join all threads in the client worker pool (in stop_id order)
1409   // This handles the data reception thread and (eventually) all other worker threads
1410   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 13. About to call thread_pool_stop_all");
1411   if (g_client_worker_pool) {
1412     asciichat_error_t result = thread_pool_stop_all(g_client_worker_pool);
1413     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1414       log_error("Failed to stop client worker threads");
1415       LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Thread pool stop failed");
1416     }
1417   }
1418   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 14. thread_pool_stop_all() returned");
1419 
1420   g_data_thread_created = false;
1421 
1422 #ifdef DEBUG_THREADS
1423   log_debug("Data reception thread stopped and joined by thread pool");
1424 #endif
1425   log_debug("[PROTOCOL_STOP] 15. protocol_stop_connection complete");
1426 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, atomic_load_bool(),
    atomic_load_u64(), audio_stop_thread(),
    capture_stop_thread(), g_client_worker_pool,
    g_h265_encoder, GET_OPTION, h265_encoder_flush(),
    keepalive_stop_thread(), log_debug, LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET,
    log_error, log_info, log_warn,
    platform_sleep_us(), SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC,
    server_connection_get_transport(),
    server_connection_is_active(), server_connection_shutdown(),
    thread_pool_stop_all(), and US_PER_MS_INT.
